species.role: second native canopy stratum, north/east dry-slope grove, and conifer silhouette accent

authority_sources.title: Reserva Nacional Futaleufu
authority_sources.authority: local forest type, exposure, grove form, and southern-range context
facts_used: The species forms locally near-pure groves in the north of the reserve.
Those groves are associated with north and east exposures.
The Futaleufu reserve is at the species' southern distribution limit.

authority_sources.title: Reserva Nacional Futaleufu - ecosystems
authority_sources.publisher: Ministerio del Medio Ambiente de Chile, SIMBIO
authority_sources.authority: local watershed association and protected-area ecosystem inventory
facts_used: Austrocedrus chilensis and Nothofagus dombeyi form a mapped evergreen Andean forest association.
The association occupies 1293.181 hectares, or 9.875 percent, of the listed protected-area ecosystem inventory.
The reserve lies in the Rio Futaleufu watershed and Valdivian temperate forest ecoregion.

authority_sources.title: Austrocedrus chilensis native-species dossier
authority_sources.authority: species morphology, national range, elevation, and habitat
facts_used: The Chilean range extends through Los Lagos, principally in the Andean foothills and cordillera.
The reported elevation range is approximately 400 to 1500 meters, with greatest abundance from 700 to 1200 meters.
Trees are evergreen and may reach 25 meters tall and 2 meters trunk diameter.
Bark is rough, brown low on the trunk, and ash-gray above.
Opposite overlapping scale leaves are dimorphic, with lateral leaves up to 3 millimeters and facial leaves about 1 millimeter.

link_only_visual_references.title: Austrocedrus chilensis
link_only_visual_references.use: temporary human review of dense feathery scale-leaf sprays, red-brown twigs, compact crown, and rough bark

link_only_visual_references.title: Austrocedrus chilensis description and photo index
link_only_visual_references.use: temporary human review of dense flat shoots and red-brown thread-peeling bark

morphology_donor_audit.title: Fir Tree 01
morphology_donor_audit.candidate_use: connected trunk and branch hierarchy donor only; original needle identity and fir ecology are rejected for native cypress use

morphology_donor_audit.title: Western Red Cedar Tree #01
morphology_donor_audit.candidate_use: Cupressaceae flattened scale-leaf morphology comparison and possible reconstruction donor after license and source review

morphology_donor_audit.title: Cypress Tree Collection
morphology_donor_audit.candidate_use: photogrammetry quality and branch-material comparison only; generic cypress form is not accepted as Austrocedrus identity

provenance_and_rights_boundary.stored_material: source URLs, publishers, retrieval dates, and bounded factual summaries only
provenance_and_rights_boundary.future_asset_ownership: project-authored assets or explicitly rights-reviewed donor geometry reconstructed to the native source contract with provenance retained
provenance_and_rights_boundary.field_reference_requirement: guide or first-party field imagery should validate corridor frequency and visual phenotype before production promotion
provenance_and_rights_boundary.source_limit: protected-area and species sources establish local plausibility, not exact tree locations along the runnable corridor

ecological_placement_contract.distribution: clustered near-pure groves with sparse coigue transition edges, never uniform one-for-one mixing
excluded_bands: south- and west-facing moist coigue-dominant slope

asset_contract.bark: rough vertically fibrous brown lower trunk transitioning toward ash gray above
asset_contract.foliage_representation: small overlapping scale leaves authored as layered branch sprays, not broadleaf cards or individually modeled millimeter leaves
asset_contract.near_lod: modeled primary and secondary branches with masked multi-spray foliage clusters
asset_contract.mid_lod: reduced branch structure with merged alpha-preserving spray clusters
asset_contract.runtime_far_lod: registered sparse silhouette clusters plus trunk, measured before corridor use
texture_requirements: project-owned periodic bark albedo, normal, and AO-roughness-height maps
project-owned non-overlapping cypress branch-spray atlas with opacity, normal, roughness, and subsurface inputs
tile-bounded transparent RGB mip padding from the first generated version
neutral material defaults with all corridor tuning exposed through instances
asset_contract.shadow_policy: disabled for initial corridor ecology captures; any later shadow-pass density or proxy must pass V23 off-crown and crown-photometry gates
asset_contract.collision_policy: visual HISM review instances remain transient and non-colliding; gameplay collision authority is unchanged

v16_procedural_vegetation_workflow.source_graph_use: transient local reconstruction scaffold only; the installed graph, preset, twig meshes, materials, textures, and generated derivatives are not copied into the open-source repository
project_owned_inputs: V10 cypress broad-spray atlas and material maps
three layered live-twig card meshes generated locally from the V10 atlas
one connected dead-twig mesh generated locally from project geometry
cypress bark and V16 masked TwoSidedFoliage materials
graph_adaptation: replace both Epic foliage palettes with project-owned live and dead twig meshes
bypass the sample-specific Manual Edit node before meshing
record per-form branch scale, axillary growth, graft density and range, senescence, random direction, gravity, bifurcation, foliage density, and seed
redirect export into a git-ignored local review namespace
use Nanite with shape preservation disabled for connected woody geometry and traditional raster rendering for masked micro-foliage

retained_controls: 2.10 live-spray base-color scale with 0.08 AO influence and zero diagnostic emissive
stronger physically lit transmission with a 0.42 opacity-mask clip
live-foliage shadows disabled only in isolated review while connected woody shadows remain
per-form gravity strength and angle correction
higher retained-foliage threshold, bounded graft density, deterministic axil jitter, and phyllotaxy offset
deterministic upper-crown spray taper applied identically to the local foliage template and review map
observed_failures: the spruce scaffold produces sparse dotted apexes
healthy forms retain stacked crown islands, open bands, or coarse shelves
the storm form remains too skeletal for normal-canopy substitution

open_visual_failures: the installed spruce scaffold produces sparse dotted apexes and crown islands after the bounded photometry/topology correction
healthy forms retain open bands or coarse branch shelves
the installed spruce scaffold is not final Austrocedrus species identity
only three diagnostic views per form exist; the V13 exact 67-camera contract has not run
corridor ecology, temporal wind, packaged desktop, and target-VR gates have not run

v17_project_graph_workflow.source_graph_use: project-owned grower, grafting, gravity, bone-reduction, and foliage-distribution topology; no installed sample topology, preset, manual-edit node, or export node is duplicated
project_owned_inputs: V10 cypress broad-spray atlas and material maps
three layered live-twig card meshes generated locally from the V10 atlas
one connected dead-twig mesh available only to damaged-form paths
cypress bark and V16 masked TwoSidedFoliage materials
accepted_outcomes: installed spruce graph topology removed from the forward authoring path
open-grown height and crown-width envelope are plausible for the 25 meter species authority
crown continuity and pyramidal mass are materially improved over the V16 scaffold
project-owned branch-spray source material and alpha response pass isolated closeup review
open_visual_failures: whole-spray card edges remain visible at the 60 meter river-view distance
the seven remaining forms have not been regenerated from the V17 graph
the V13 exact 67-camera and V10 near/mid/far authority gates have not run
temporal wind, corridor ecology, packaged desktop, and target-VR gates have not run
the basal flare remains a secondary isolated-review cleanup item

isolated: two adult and one intermediate turntable azimuth per form
no broadleaf identity, opaque card walls, black branch marks, or repeated conical clones
corridor: guide-seat, river-eye, and high-bank ecology captures
coigue transition edge and near-pure grove review
packaged desktop and on-device VR performance
guide or local ecology review of abundance and placement
automatic_reject_conditions: placement below 400 meters without direct field evidence
placement without a verified true-north transform
external source pixels or geometry copied without redistribution approval
shadow treatment that reintroduces V15 ground carpets or V23 crown collapse
production claim based only on protected-area plausibility

next_step: Keep the V17 project-owned growth graph and solve the visible whole-spray card edges through a validated near/mid/far geometry, card, or impostor authority path. Regenerate the seven remaining forms only after the open-grown representation passes its 60 meter visual gate, then run the V13 exact 67-camera, V10 authority, temporal wind, corridor ecology, packaged desktop, and target-VR gates. Keep installed sample content and local generated derivatives out of the open-source repository.
